jokes.text: I'm perfectly civil. He's the stupid garbage head doodoo face.
jokes.setup: Leslie claims she's perfectly civil when dealing with her nemesis
jokes.punchline: Immediately follows with childish name-calling
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie Knope character moment - the immediate contradiction between her claim of civility and juvenile insult is classic Parks and Rec humor. Strong structure and economy.

jokes.text: quote, 'It's time to trade those dumb old trees for a buttload of cash.'
jokes.setup: Formal legal announcement from prestigious Newport family
jokes.punchline: Crude, informal language in official quote
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between formal setting and casual vulgarity. Establishes Jessica's character efficiently.

jokes.text: It is. It truly is.
jokes.setup: Trevor reads her crude quote about trading trees for cash
jokes.punchline: Jessica's earnest, serious agreement with her own ridiculous statement
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character beat showing Jessica's oblivious sincerity about her materialistic values.

jokes.text: I'm gonna work until I'm 100 and then cut back to four days a week. Oh, God. I'm already so bored thinking about that one day off. Maybe I'll go to law school or something.
jokes.setup: Leslie talks about retirement
jokes.punchline: Escalating absurdity of her workaholic nature
jokes.explanation: Pure Leslie Knope character work - the escalation from working until 100 to being bored by one day off perfectly captures her manic energy.

jokes.text: Hello, Miss Knope. / Hello, former strange person I used to friend.
jokes.setup: Ron's polite greeting to Leslie
jokes.punchline: Leslie's awkward attempt to describe their former relationship
jokes.explanation: Good awkward Leslie moment - 'used to friend' as a verb shows her discomfort in a characteristically verbose way.

jokes.text: You're looking very Ron-like. / You have your same hair. / No, I don't! I have bangs now! / I've never known what bangs are, and I don't intend to learn!
jokes.setup: Awkward small talk about appearance
jokes.punchline: Ron's aggressive rejection of learning about bangs
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron Swanson moment - his militant ignorance about women's hairstyles fits his character perfectly.

jokes.text: It's called Very Good Building and Development Company. I wanted to convey the quality of our work without seeming flashy.
jokes.setup: Ron explains his company name
jokes.punchline: The absurdly literal, unimaginative name presented as thoughtful branding
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson humor - his completely literal approach to business naming, delivered with complete seriousness, is both absurd and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: The trees and rocks and the sky. / Yes. The land has good sky.
jokes.setup: Craig's overly enthusiastic description of the land
jokes.punchline: Ron's matter-of-fact agreement about the sky quality
jokes.explanation: Good Ron moment - treating 'good sky' as a legitimate real estate feature with complete seriousness.

jokes.text: This guy, he's so far out. So normcore.
jokes.setup: Craig describing Ron
jokes.punchline: Using contradictory tech buzzwords to describe the least tech-savvy person
jokes.explanation: Decent juxtaposition of tech speak with Ron's anti-technology character, though not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: This is like Morningstar all over again! / This is nothing like Morningstar. / And that was two years ago.
jokes.setup: Leslie brings up past grievance
jokes.punchline: Ron's dismissive correction about timeline
jokes.explanation: Sets up ongoing tension and hints at their complicated history, though not laugh-out-loud funny.

jokes.text: Leslie! I couldn't find that file you wanted, but I did find a file called Bird Census 1980. And it's empty. / Get out of here, Ed! I fired you. / Right. If anybody wants to hang, I will be at Subway.
jokes.setup: Leslie claims to have a large staff
jokes.punchline: Ed appears as her only 'employee' who she's already fired
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Leslie's boasts - the immediate contradiction of her 'staff' claims, plus Ed's oblivious offer to hang out.

jokes.text: Get out of here, Ed! I fired you. Right. If anybody wants to hang, I will be at Subway.
jokes.setup: Leslie fires Ed
jokes.punchline: Ed casually invites everyone to hang out at Subway despite being fired
jokes.explanation: Ed's obliviousness creates awkward humor. The Subway detail adds specificity.

jokes.text: He's very stupid.
jokes.setup: Ed's bizarre behavior
jokes.punchline: Leslie's matter-of-fact assessment to camera
jokes.explanation: Simple deadpan reaction, effective but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Babe, I just dropped off Leslie's triplets with her mom. All three of 'em. Nailed it.
jokes.setup: Andy reporting on a simple task
jokes.punchline: His proud celebration of basic counting
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy moment - treating basic competence as a major achievement fits his lovable idiot character.

jokes.text: Welcome back to the Johnny Karate Super Awesome Musical Explosion Show.
jokes.setup: Andy has his own TV show
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous, over-the-top name
jokes.explanation: Fits Andy's character but the 'random = funny' approach is somewhat lazy.

jokes.text: Andy, no. / Ninjas attack! / Andy. / No. That's my crotch. / Okay, guys. Three more kicks apiece.
jokes.setup: Kids show scenario
jokes.punchline: Andy getting kicked in the crotch and asking for more
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy, fits Andy but relies on cheap crotch humor.

jokes.text: Oh, wow! / I know! I am just... I am so happy for me!
jokes.setup: Leslie expressing surprise at the land's value
jokes.punchline: Jessica's complete self-centeredness in response
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Jessica's narcissism, though relatively straightforward.

jokes.text: Oh, Leslie, I have always liked you. / Yeah, no, you haven't though.
jokes.setup: Jessica's false claim of friendship
jokes.punchline: Leslie's blunt correction
jokes.explanation: Good moment of honesty from Leslie, cuts through social niceties efficiently.

jokes.text: So, between money and Leslie... You see the bind that I'm in.
jokes.setup: Jessica explaining her difficult choice
jokes.punchline: Treating money vs. doing good as equally weighted options
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Jessica's complete obliviousness to how obvious her choice is.

jokes.text: Who is Tom Haverford? He's a mentor, a lover, a hero. But who is my hero? Simple. It's me five years from now.
jokes.setup: Interviewer asks simple question about spelling
jokes.punchline: Tom's grandiose self-description
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom Haverford moment - the escalating narcissism from simple question to philosophical self-aggrandizement.

jokes.text: You are looking at one of Indiana Business Monthly's 35 under 35. I own Pawnee's hottest restaurant, two fast casual eateries, and the Tommy Chopper. We serve chopped salads out of a decommissioned military helicopter.
jokes.setup: Tom listing his business achievements
jokes.punchline: The absurd helicopter salad restaurant
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist escalation - the helicopter restaurant is creative, unexpected, and perfectly fits Tom's over-the-top business ventures.

jokes.text: Wanna wake me up when you're done boring us to death, am I right, Janet? / Write down that I'm funny.
jokes.setup: Tom insults Ben to the interviewer
jokes.punchline: Immediately asking to have his humor documented
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about self-awareness, fits Tom's character perfectly.

jokes.text: It's my biggest project since Ice Town, so it really means a lot that I'm being recognized as a city leader instead of being yelled at and pelted with things.
jokes.setup: Ben talking about being honored
jokes.punchline: Reference to his traumatic past failure
jokes.explanation: Good character moment referencing Ben's defining backstory, shows growth while acknowledging trauma.

jokes.text: Wait. You don't think this is all an elaborate setup to pelt me with things, do you?
jokes.setup: Ben's previous comment about being pelted
jokes.punchline: His genuine paranoia about it happening again
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation of Ben's Ice Town trauma, showing how it still affects him.

jokes.text: Andy, we're planning our whole week, like old people! We used to be spontaneous and weird. We used to eat cereal out of Frisbees because we didn't have any bowls.
jokes.setup: April realizing they've become domestic
jokes.punchline: Specific example of their former chaos
jokes.explanation: Good character development moment - the Frisbee detail perfectly captures their former chaotic lifestyle.

jokes.text: You once broke a rib trying to wrestle one of those inflatable floppy guys at a car dealership. / I won.
jokes.setup: April describing Andy's past recklessness
jokes.punchline: Andy's proud claim of victory
jokes.explanation: Excellent Andy moment - the absurd scenario is perfectly in character, and his prideful 'I won' is the perfect punchline.

jokes.text: Well, I only have $2 million in the discretionary fund, and I've already used some of it to make Thomas Jefferson sexier in those Mount Rushmore promotions. Which, by the way, attendance has been up. That's not a coincidence.
jokes.setup: April explaining budget constraints
jokes.punchline: Using federal funds to sexualize Thomas Jefferson
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor - the idea of government funds being used to make historical figures 'sexier' is both unexpected and perfectly fits April's subversive nature.

jokes.text: I have the most valuable currency in America, a blind, stubborn belief that what I am doing is 100% right.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining how she'll compete despite having less money
jokes.punchline: Her self-aware description of her main character trait
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work - Leslie's complete self-awareness about her stubborn nature while being completely unrepentant about it. Highly quotable and perfectly captures her character.

jokes.text: My son sells them on Etsy. He is a huge disappointment.
jokes.setup: Ken explaining where his bolo tie came from
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact assessment of his son
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan delivery - the casual brutality of calling his son a 'huge disappointment' over bolo tie sales is perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: So you would like the Wamapoke people to pay the federal government millions of dollars to buy land that was stolen from us by the federal government? And we don't even get to keep the land?
jokes.setup: Leslie asking for donation to buy the land
jokes.punchline: Ken pointing out the absurd irony of the request
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy that highlights real historical injustices while being genuinely funny. The logical breakdown of Leslie's request is both educational and hilarious.

jokes.text: What if I buy some of your disappointing son's bolo ties? Damn it.
jokes.setup: Leslie's desperate attempt to salvage the conversation
jokes.punchline: Callback to the son being disappointing
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Leslie's desperation while maintaining the running gag about Ken's son.

jokes.text: I'm gonna take a Zantac. All that salt will give me heartburn. / Oh, God. You ruined it.
jokes.setup: Andy about to do something wild and spontaneous
jokes.punchline: His responsible health planning ruins the moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of how they've become responsible adults - Andy's practical health consideration completely undermines their attempt at spontaneity.

jokes.text: Oh, actually, Ron promised me I could build a restaurant on the new Gryzzl campus. So I'm kind of totally on Ron's side.
jokes.setup: Leslie rallying her old team
jokes.punchline: Tom's immediate defection for personal gain
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tom's opportunistic nature, though predictable.

jokes.text: Yeah. Ron hired Regal Meagle Realty to broker the deal. And I'm gonna need the money for my wedding. Shia LaBeouf designed wedding dresses do not come cheap.
jokes.setup: Donna explaining why she's on Ron's side
jokes.punchline: Shia LaBeouf as wedding dress designer
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist detail - Shia LaBeouf designing wedding dresses is unexpected and fits the show's random celebrity references.

jokes.text: Well, never fear, because Terry is here. Yes, I go by Terry now. Because there was a guy at National Parks. He was already named Larry, so they suggested I go by Terry. And then I said my real name is Gary, and they said, 'Who cares?' So, it's just a fun group.
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining his name change
jokes.punchline: The bureaucratic absurdity and his positive spin on being dismissed
jokes.explanation: Excellent continuation of the Jerry name gag - the escalating confusion and his oblivious positivity about being dismissed is perfect character work.

jokes.text: Unless you're talking about a map of the cities with the most obese pets.
jokes.setup: Tom talks about Pawnee not being on the map
jokes.punchline: Except for this very specific and unflattering statistic
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline structure. The obese pets detail is specific enough to be funny.

jokes.text: And that man was me. Tom Haverford, owner and proprietor of the effortlessly chic, celebrity-packed Italian restaurant, Tom's Bistro.
jokes.setup: Tom supposedly introducing Ben as the man who lifted up the town
jokes.punchline: Immediately making it about himself and his restaurant
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom narcissism - turning what should be Ben's moment into self-promotion is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I simply took a skewer for one shrimp and added many shrimp to it. They're long enough for five or six. / I fit seven on this one. It's a masterpiece.
jokes.setup: Leslie confronting Ron about stealing her team
jokes.punchline: Ron's complete focus on optimizing shrimp skewers
jokes.explanation: Good Ron moment - his complete obliviousness to the drama while focusing on practical efficiency is very in character.

jokes.text: People come up to me, and they say things like, 'Tom, given all that you've accomplished, is it hard to stay humble?' And I say, 'Not for me. I'm pretty amazing at being humble.'
jokes.setup: Tom talking about staying humble
jokes.punchline: His boastful claim about being amazing at humility
jokes.explanation: Classic paradox humor - being proud of humility is a perfect Tom moment that's both absurd and completely believable for him.

jokes.text: And thank you to the Boys and Girls Club for the incredibly life-like papier-mâché statue.
jokes.setup: Ben giving thanks at the gala
jokes.punchline: The sarcastic description of a clearly poor quality statue
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from the contrast between polite thanks and obvious quality issues, fits Ben's diplomatic nature.

jokes.text: I told you to never say that word to me! No one should ever say that word out loud. It's like 'Voldemort' or 'Ron.'
jokes.setup: Leslie's extreme reaction to 'Morningstar'
jokes.punchline: Comparing Ron's name to Voldemort
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Leslie's hatred for Ron, the Voldemort comparison adds pop culture wit to her anger.

jokes.text: Even Leslie is crazier than we are. They're still gonna serve that cake, right?
jokes.setup: April observes that Leslie fighting over cake is crazier than their planned streaking
jokes.punchline: Andy's immediate concern about whether they'll still get cake
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy priority - despite the chaos, he's focused on cake. Good economy and character work.

jokes.text: I love you, too. I love your skin. Give me your skin.
jokes.setup: Jessica expressing love for her new tablet
jokes.punchline: AI's creepy, threatening response
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor about AI gone wrong - the sudden turn to demanding skin is genuinely creepy and funny.

jokes.text: And then we need to sign a suicide pact because our lives are meaningless.
jokes.setup: April listing their boring adult responsibilities
jokes.punchline: Dramatic escalation to suicide pact
jokes.explanation: Perfect April humor - her dramatic overreaction to mundane adult tasks with dark comedy fits her character perfectly.

jokes.text: Where are we? I have never once been to this part of Pawnee. It's the creepy warehouse district. It's mostly just raccoons and old car batteries.
jokes.setup: Andy asks where they are
jokes.punchline: April's description of the area as populated only by raccoons and car batteries
jokes.explanation: Good specific details that paint a picture of urban decay. The raccoons and batteries pairing is nicely absurd.

jokes.text: Oh, boo-hoo! It all ran off into the lake. Well, yes, admittedly, it did make the fish taste delicious, but it was a PR nightmare.
jokes.setup: Leslie mentions the hot fudge pipeline disaster
jokes.punchline: Jessica dismisses environmental concerns but admits the fish tasted good
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jessica - dismissive of criticism but finding the silver lining in chocolate-flavored fish. The casual 'boo-hoo' is perfectly bratty.

jokes.text: It says here there are 12 closets, three bomb shelters, five dumbwaiters, two and three-eighths baths, no kitchens. It's a fairly standard layout.
jokes.setup: Realtor describing the house features
jokes.punchline: Calling this bizarre layout 'standard'
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist humor - the completely insane house layout presented as normal, plus the specific '3/8 baths' detail.

jokes.text: Remember the Pawnee Doll Head Factory? This was a doll head factory? No. This was a holding cell for people who went insane on the assembly line.
jokes.setup: Explanation of what the building used to be
jokes.punchline: It wasn't the factory but the asylum for the workers
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy with great misdirection - the reveal that it's even worse than expected is both funny and disturbing.

jokes.text: I'm Attorney Trevor Nelsson with the law firm Fwar, Dips, Winshares, Gritt, Babip, Pecota, Vorp and Eckstein
jokes.setup: Formal legal announcement
jokes.punchline: Law firm name that's clearly baseball statistics terms
jokes.explanation: Good nerd humor for baseball fans, though may go over many viewers' heads. Creative but not universally accessible.

jokes.text: In the words of Jason Bourne, 'This is where it started for me. This is where it ends.' / You know, I still think Kevin James was a weird choice for the reboot.
jokes.setup: Leslie quoting Jason Bourne dramatically
jokes.punchline: Ben's response about Kevin James playing Bourne
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor - the image of Kevin James as Jason Bourne is perfectly ridiculous, and Ben's casual delivery makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: And also I made cookies, but Ron is not allowed to eat them and they say, 'Prepare for War' on them, but the O in the word 'for' is a heart.
jokes.setup: Leslie's war declaration
jokes.punchline: The contradictory sweetness in her aggressive cookies
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - the contradiction between declaring war and making heart-decorated cookies captures her character beautifully.

jokes.text: And also I made cookies, but Ron is not allowed to eat them and they say, 'Prepare for War' on them, but the O in the word 'for' is a heart.
jokes.setup: Leslie declares war on her former team
jokes.punchline: Her war cookies have hearts in them, showing she can't fully commit to being mean
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie - even her aggressive gestures are undermined by her fundamental kindness. The heart detail is wonderfully specific character work.

tmdb_overview: Leslie launches a competition with tech company Gryzzl for a large parcel of land, which she wants to turn into a National Park. April and Andy try to regain their spontaneity. Ben is recognized at a Bicentennial gala, where Tom delivers a speech in his honor.
insight: Seven-year time jump lands 52 jokes in 44 minutes—deadpan understatement carries the reboot.
